_note: Ground truth for the four natural-language workshop questions.
expected_graph_proven_ids — ids that MUST appear in the run's
Use 'minimum' for open-ended impact-style questions where a broader
reading can still be 'correct'; use 'exact' when the question maps

Q1.question_hint: If UNECE R155 is removed, which FRs/DRs are affected?
Q1.rationale: The impact set is exactly Engine start/stop (Function Requirement) directly derived from UNECE R155 via its Req Trace Item (SP0003+SP0006), plus two Design Requirements one SP0006 hop further. The planner drops structural_containment for Legal Requirement roots, so the packet itself is narrow and the answer is deterministic.

Q2.rationale: Yes — one ITRQ-direct Test Case ('Keyless entry Test Case') plus four Test Cases linked via Test Specification 'Key mangement' (ITTR to requirement, ITEC to each Test Case).

Q3.question_hint: Downstream trace tree of the stakeholder requirement 'Unauthorized start detection'.
Q3.rationale: Local downstream trace gives three Function Requirements (Diagnostic communication manager, Alarm for unauthorized entry, Authentication time) via the stakeholder's own Req Trace Item, plus three Design Requirements one further hop (Trigger alarm for unauthorized start, Activate acoustic alarm, Limit authentication time). Broader readings that add peripheral FRs reached via Function Specification are tolerated.

Q4.question_hint: If 'Engine start time' is tightened, which DRs are impacted?
Q4.rationale: Two Design Requirements directly impacted via the FR's own Req Trace Item: 'Ensure timely engine start' ('within N time') and 'Apply engine start timeout' ('predefined timeout'). Both have timing/timeout explicit.
